questions.question: Indian Institute of Science (IISc) is situated in which city?
questions.explanation: IISc is the premier institute for advanced scientific and technological research and education in India. It is located in Bangalore city in India, and was established in the year 1909.

questions.question: Commission for Scientific and Technical Terminology was set up in the year?
questions.explanation: Commission for Scientific and Technical Terminology was set up on 1st October, 1961 by a resolution of Government of India, Ministry of Education.

questions.question: Which among the following organizations/institutions is located in Dehradun?
questions.explanation: The Indian Military Academy is located in Dehradun. It is the officer training Academy of the Indian Army. IMA was established in 1932.

questions.question: RFRI is situated in Jorhat, Assam. What does "F" stand for?
questions.explanation: Rain Forest Research Institute (RFRI) is a Research institute situated in Jorhat in Assam. It works under the Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education.

questions.question: National Sanskrit Vidyapeeth is situated in ____________?
questions.explanation: Rashtriya Sanskrit Vidyapeetha, a University established under section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956 is a premier instituion in the field of higher learning in Sanskrit studies. The vidyapeetha was established at Tirupati (A.P.) in 1961.

questions.question: Sahitya Akademi was founded in the year _______?
questions.explanation: The Sahitya Akademi, India's National Academy of Letters, is an organisation dedicated to the promotion of literature in the languages of India. Founded on 12 March 1954.

questions.question: National Test House in Kolkata has totally how many regional branches?
questions.explanation: It has six of its regional branches located at Kolkata, Mumbai, Chennai, Ghaziabad, Jaipur and Guwahati. In all these branches testing and evaluation facilities of finished products/raw materials of all the branches of engineering disciplines are available under one roof.

questions.question: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Police Academy is located in ________?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Police Academy is the Indian national institute for training of Indian Police Service officers. The academy is in Hyderabad, India.

questions.question: IBM is situated in Nagpur. What does "M" stand for?
questions.explanation: Indian Bureau of Mines was set up on 1st March,1948. It is situated in Nagpur.

questions.question: National Institute of Homoeopathy is located in ________?
questions.explanation: National Institute of Homoeopathy (NIH) is an autonomous organization situated in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.question: Which among the following institute related to welfare is situated in Dehradun?
options: National Institute for the Visually Handicapped
National Rehabilitation Training and Research Institute
questions.explanation: The National Institute for the Empowerment of Persons with Visual Disabilities (Divyangjan), is a premier Institute in the field of visual disability working under the administrative control of Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India. It is situated in Dehradun.

questions.question: National Thermal Power Corporation is situated in ___________?
questions.explanation: NTPC Limited is India's largest energy conglomerate established in 1975 to accelerate power development in India. It is headquartered in New Delhi.

questions.question: BRBRAITT is located in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh. It is associated with ______?
questions.explanation: BRBRAITT, Jabalpur is the apex training institute of Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited. BRBRAITT stands for Bharat Ratna Bhim Rao Ambedkar Institute of Telecom Training.

questions.question: Indian Institute of Tourism and Travel Management is situated in ___________?
questions.explanation: Indian Institute of Tourism and Travel Management (IITTM) is an institute based in Gwalior, Madhya Pradesh, India.

questions.question: Saha Nuclear Physics Institute is in which state?
questions.explanation: The Saha Institute of Nuclear Physics is for basic research and training in physical and biophysical sciences located in Kolkata, India. The institute is named after the Indian physicist Meghnad Saha.

questions.question: National Labour Institute, under the Ministry of Labour in Noida, Uttar Pradesh is named after which among the following Indian personality?
questions.explanation: V.V.Giri National Labour Institute, under the Ministry of Labour of Government of India, is an autonomous institute dedicated to the research in the field of labour and training of labour administrators, trade unions, public sector managers and other government functionaries concerned with labour. National Labour Institute till 1995, it was renamed to its present name in the honour of former President of India V.V. Giri.

questions.question: Tropical Forest Research Institute is situated in ___________?
questions.explanation: Tropical Forest Research Institute is situated in Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh. It works under the Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education.

questions.question: ICPR is situated in New Delhi. What does "P" stand for?
questions.explanation: The Indian Council of Philosophical Research (ICPR) was set up by the Ministry of Education, Government of India in March 1977.

questions.question: Central Arid Zone Research Institute was established in which city of Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: The Central Arid Zone Research Institute was established by the Government of India in 1952 in Jodhpur, Rajasthan. It was previously known as Desert Afforestation Research Station until it was renamed in 1959.

questions.question: What is the present name of Institute of Armament Studies?
options: Military College of Electrical and Mechanical Engineering
questions.explanation: The Defence Institute of Advanced Technology, (DIAT) as it is known today, came into being as the Institute of Armament Studies in 1952 in Pune.

questions.question: Institute of Asian Studies is named after which among the following personality?
questions.explanation: The Maulana Abul Kalam Azad Institute of Asian Studies was set up at the joint initiative of the Government of India, Department of culture, Ministry of Human Resource Development and the Government of West Bengal. He became the first Minister of Education in the Indian government.

questions.question: Central Institute of Buddhist Studies is situated in _________?
questions.explanation: The Central Institute of Buddhist Studies, formerly known as the School of Buddhist Philosophy, is located in Leh, Jammu & Kashmir. It was founded in 1959.

questions.question: NRLC in Lucknow is related to __________?
questions.explanation: National Research Laboratory for Conservation of Cultural Property (NRLC), established in 1976 under the Ministry of Culture, is the premier organization for the researches in conservation of the cultural property including monuments and sites, as well as museums, library and archive collections.

questions.question: Which among the following institute is situated in Chennai?
questions.explanation: National Institute of Siddha is a premier institute of Siddha medicine situated at Tambaram, Chennai, India.

questions.question: Sangeet Natak Akademi comes under which Ministry?
questions.explanation: Sangeet Natak Akademi is the national level academy for performing arts set up by the Government of India under Ministry of Culture.

questions.question: National Judicial Academy is situated in ___________?
questions.explanation: National Judicial Academy is an Indian government-funded training institute primarily for Judicial Officers, working in the Supreme Court and the High Courts, during their service. It is loacated in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh.

questions.question: National Film Archive of India was established in the year __________?
questions.explanation: The National Film Archive of India was established as a media unit of the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting in February 1964.

questions.question: National Institute of Criminology & Forensic Science was set up in ________?
questions.explanation: The National Institute of Criminology & Forensic Science was set up in January 1972 as a comprehensive facility for teaching, training, research and consultancy in the fields of Criminology & Forensic Science to meet the ground level requirements of the Criminal Justice Administration of the country. The Institute functions under the Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India as an attached office.

questions.question: Indian Institute of Astrophysics is located in _________?
questions.explanation: The Indian Institute of Astrophysics, with its headquarters in Bangalore, is a premier National Research Institute of India.

questions.question: National Centre for Basic Sciences in Kolkata is named after _______?
questions.explanation: S. N. Bose National Centre for Basic Sciences is an Autonomous Research Institute established under Department of Science and Technology, Government of India in 1986 as a Registered Society. The Centre was established to honour the life and work of Professor S. N. Bose.
